evidence.page_title: Natural gas pipeline ruptures, explodes in Webb County - KGNS
evidence.title: Natural gas pipeline ruptures, explodes in Webb County
evidence.description: A natural gas pipeline ruptured and exploded on Hwy 255, prompting a response from Webb County Fire Department crews.
evidence.text:
Natural gas pipeline ruptures, explodes in Webb County
WEBB COUNTY, Tex. (KGNS) - A natural gas pipeline ruptured and exploded on Hwy 255, prompting a response from Webb County Fire Department crews.
The Webb County Fire Department reported the incident just before 11 a.m. on March 20. Fire units were en route to another call when they responded to the scene.
Fire crews established a secure perimeter around the active fire. Currently, the fire is being monitored under controlled conditions while utility personnel work to shut off the gas supply.
Fire suppression efforts will be initiated once the natural gas flow is stopped, according to the fire department.
Officials said this is the safest and most effective approach to prevent further hazards.
No injuries have been reported.
At just after 12 p.m., Webb County Fire Chief Ramiro Elizondo Jr. reported that two pipelines from Howard Energy and Lewis Energy Group had ruptured.
He noted that the valves had been shut off and the remaining natural gas had burned off following the rupture. Crews are now working to extinguish the hot spots caused by the explosion.
Currently, no further damage has been reported, and there is no danger to the surrounding area.
For more headlines, click here.
Copyright 2026 KGNS. All rights reserved.

evidence.page_title: Large natural gas pipeline explosion in Ellsworth County - WIBW
evidence.title: Large natural gas pipeline explosion in Ellsworth County
evidence.description: A large natural gas pipeline explosion occurred after 1:30 p.m. Thursday in Ellsworth County.
evidence.text:
Large natural gas pipeline explosion in Ellsworth County
ELLSWORTH COUNTY, Kan. (KWCH) - A pipeline owned and operated by Northern Natural Gas exploded in Ellsworth County.
A spokesperson says the explosion was started by a gas leak. The explosion occurred near 23rd Road and Avenue D.
The gas company is currently working with first responders and there is no threat to public safety.
The gas has been shut off to the line and the fire will continue to burn out.
Service interruptions are not expected.
The same gas company has issues with a well pad at the company’s natural gas underground storage facility near Lyons last week, causing a gas smell in the town.
A large natural gas pipeline explosion occurred after 1:30 p.m. Thursday in Ellsworth County.
According to KHP Trooper Ben Gardner, the explosion occurred on I-70 near mile marker 222. A fire continues at the site of the explosion. The explosion occurred in a pasture.
Eyewitness News has a crew headed to the scene to gather more information.
Copyright 2021 KWCH. All rights reserved.
